name: Nucleon axial charge g_A from lattice QCD
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the nucleon axial charge g_A, which controls the rate of neutron beta decay. The CalLat lattice-QCD result g_A = 1.271 +/- 0.013 (1.0% precision) agrees with the experimental value g_A = 1.2756 +/- 0.0013 (PDG 2024) at ~1 sigma; an important benchmark for first-principles non-perturbative QCD.

regime.description: Nucleon scale; non-perturbative matrix element of the axial current.

current_bound.experiment: CalLat lattice + ultracold-neutron decay asymmetry experiments (UCNA, PERKEO III)
current_bound.reference: Chang et al. (CalLat), 'A per-cent-level determination of the nucleon axial coupling from quantum chromodynamics', Nature 558, 91 (2018), arXiv:1805.12130; Markisch et al. (PERKEO III), PRL 122, 242501 (2019)

what_it_excludes: Establishes that lattice QCD reproduces fundamental nucleon properties at the percent level. Constrains BSM modifications of the axial-vector quark current (e.g. right-handed W couplings, leptoquarks) that would shift g_A.

parameterization.name: Direct: g_A = matrix element <p| u_bar gamma^mu gamma_5 d |n>, normalized so that the SM beta-decay matrix element is proportional to (1 + 3 g_A^2).
parameterization.reference: Chang et al. (CalLat), Nature 558, 91 (2018); PDG 2024 review of nucleon properties.
parameterization.summary: g_A computed by extracting the asymptotic plateau of three-point correlation functions on the lattice; controlled by chiral and continuum extrapolations.

procedural.predicted_observable: Decay correlation parameter A in polarized neutron beta decay; lifetime tau_n; combined with V_ud and lattice theory.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Lattice and experiment agree to ~1 sigma; any BSM contribution to the axial current must keep |delta g_A / g_A| < ~10^-3.
framework_inputs: If the framework modifies QCD or weak couplings to quarks, compute the resulting shift in g_A (or in V_ud * g_A as measured).

primary_references.citation: Chang et al. (CalLat), 'A per-cent-level determination of the nucleon axial coupling from quantum chromodynamics', Nature 558, 91 (2018)
Markisch et al. (PERKEO III), 'Measurement of the Weak Axial-Vector Coupling Constant in the Decay of Free Neutrons Using a Pulsed Cold Neutron Beam', PRL 122, 242501 (2019)
